instance.answer_contract: Object with "factory_a", "factory_b", and "makespan". Each factory maps machine names to lists of ["J<id>", start, end] triples.
instance.problem_statement:
You run a two-factory manufacturing pipeline. Factory A makes components, then Factory B finishes the same jobs.
Each factory is its own job shop with one-job-per-machine exclusivity and no preemption.
Every job must finish all of its Factory A operations before that same job may start any Factory B operation.
Your objective is to minimize the coupled makespan: the time when the final Factory B operation finishes.

Instance size: 6 jobs, 7 machines in each factory.
Jobs and ordered operations:
- Job 1: Factory A MA2 (5) -> MA3 (3) -> MA1 (2) -> MA4 (5) -> MA7 (4) -> MA5 (1) -> MA6 (5); Factory B MB1 (5) -> MB7 (4) -> MB4 (3) -> MB3 (5) -> MB6 (5) -> MB5 (6) -> MB2 (2)
- Job 2: Factory A MA7 (5) -> MA5 (5) -> MA3 (9) -> MA4 (4) -> MA6 (7) -> MA1 (7) -> MA2 (5); Factory B MB2 (7) -> MB4 (8) -> MB3 (3) -> MB1 (4) -> MB5 (5) -> MB7 (5) -> MB6 (1)
- Job 3: Factory A MA1 (5) -> MA7 (3) -> MA4 (6) -> MA3 (7) -> MA5 (6) -> MA2 (9) -> MA6 (4); Factory B MB2 (6) -> MB6 (2) -> MB7 (1) -> MB1 (4) -> MB5 (5) -> MB4 (4) -> MB3 (2)
- Job 4: Factory A MA3 (6) -> MA2 (3) -> MA7 (7) -> MA4 (2) -> MA1 (5) -> MA5 (4) -> MA6 (8); Factory B MB6 (5) -> MB1 (3) -> MB3 (5) -> MB5 (7) -> MB7 (3) -> MB2 (6) -> MB4 (1)
- Job 5: Factory A MA3 (1) -> MA1 (1) -> MA4 (1) -> MA2 (3) -> MA5 (3) -> MA7 (1) -> MA6 (9); Factory B MB5 (8) -> MB1 (4) -> MB4 (6) -> MB2 (1) -> MB7 (2) -> MB6 (9) -> MB3 (5)
- Job 6: Factory A MA7 (7) -> MA4 (4) -> MA2 (8) -> MA6 (4) -> MA1 (1) -> MA3 (1) -> MA5 (5); Factory B MB4 (5) -> MB7 (4) -> MB1 (9) -> MB2 (4) -> MB6 (4) -> MB3 (7) -> MB5 (5)

Constraints:
- A machine handles at most one job at a time.
- Each job must respect its listed operation order inside each factory.
- Factory B job Jx cannot start until Factory A job Jx has fully completed.
- When you emit a schedule, every required operation must appear exactly once.

A deterministic feasible baseline schedule is provided below. If you stop immediately or fail to produce a valid schedule, this baseline is what gets scored.
Baseline makespan: 197
Baseline schedule summary:
Factory A: MA1: J1[8-10],J2[49-56],J3[56-61],J4[104-109],J5[109-110],J6[141-142]; MA2: J1[0-5],J2[56-61],J3[83-92],J4[92-95],J5[111-114],J6[129-137]; MA3: J1[5-8],J2[29-38],J3[70-77],J4[77-83],J5[83-84],J6[142-143]; MA4: J1[10-15],J2[38-42],J3[64-70],J4[102-104],J5[110-111],J6[125-129]; MA5: J1[19-20],J2[24-29],J3[77-83],J4[109-113],J5[114-117],J6[143-148]; MA6: J1[20-25],J2[42-49],J3[92-96],J4[113-121],J5[121-130],J6[137-141]; MA7: J1[15-19],J2[19-24],J3[61-64],J4[95-102],J5[117-118],J6[118-125]
Factory B: MB1: J1[25-30],J2[79-83],J3[105-109],J4[126-129],J5[149-153],J6[168-177]; MB2: J1[53-55],J2[61-68],J3[96-102],J4[144-150],J5[159-160],J6[177-181]; MB3: J1[37-42],J2[76-79],J3[118-120],J4[129-134],J5[171-176],J6[185-192]; MB4: J1[34-37],J2[68-76],J3[114-118],J4[150-151],J5[153-159],J6[159-164]; MB5: J1[47-53],J2[83-88],J3[109-114],J4[134-141],J5[141-149],J6[192-197]; MB6: J1[42-47],J2[93-94],J3[102-104],J4[121-126],J5[162-171],J6[181-185]; MB7: J1[30-34],J2[88-93],J3[104-105],J4[141-144],J5[160-162],J6[164-168]
Makespan: 197
